note: Concepts, not verbatim strings. symptom_verbatim on each SymptomFinding is never normalised; this table is the reversible mapping layer. HPO IDs from the Human Phenotype Ontology; null where no adequate term exists. fatigue and post_exertional_malaise are kept as separate concepts and never merged.

symptoms.label: Fatigue
symptoms.definition_note: Persistent tiredness/lack of energy reported as a symptom. Distinct from post-exertional malaise; a paper reporting only 'fatigue' maps here, not to PEM.

symptoms.label: Post-exertional malaise
symptoms.definition_note: Worsening of symptoms after physical/cognitive exertion, often delayed. Never merged with generic fatigue even where a paper conflates them.

symptoms.label: CFS-like illness / ME/CFS caseness
symptoms.definition_note: Meeting a chronic fatigue syndrome case definition (a symptom-cluster outcome), as opposed to the single symptom of fatigue.

symptoms.label: Arthralgia / rheumatic musculoskeletal pain
symptoms.definition_note: Joint pain / rheumatic pain reported as a symptom (not radiographic arthritis).

symptoms.label: Muscle weakness
symptoms.definition_note: Reduced muscle strength reported as a symptom.

symptoms.label: Cognitive dysfunction
symptoms.definition_note: Subjective or measured impairment of concentration, processing, or executive function.

symptoms.label: Sleep disturbance
symptoms.definition_note: Any reported disturbance of sleep quality or continuity.

symptoms.label: Depression / depressed mood
symptoms.definition_note: Depressive symptoms or diagnosis reported as an outcome.

symptoms.label: Headache
symptoms.definition_note: Headache reported as a persistent symptom.

symptoms.label: Irritable bowel syndrome (post-infectious)
symptoms.definition_note: IBS meeting Rome criteria; a GI symptom-cluster outcome.
